START: ^You stand at the entrance to an ancient pyramid complex buried in jungle.
grand_entrance: ^A vast entrance hall. Columns stretch to the ceiling. Three passages ahead.
exterior_grounds: ^Overgrown grounds surrounding the pyramid. Stone blocks everywhere.
side_entrance: ^A smaller entrance on the pyramid's south face.
left_passage: ^The left passage leads to a series of chambers.
central_passage: ^The central passage is wider and more decorated.
grounds_search: ^You find old tools and a rope.
south_corridor: ^A narrow corridor running along the south face.
chamber_one: ^A chamber with relief carvings on every wall.
left_passage_end: ^The passage ends at a shaft going down.
shaft_antechamber: ^A narrow ledge just above the shaft floor. Your eyes adjust to the darkness.
decoration_study: ^The decorations depict a ritual requiring offerings and a sacred sigil.
central_hall: ^A grand hall at the centre of the pyramid. An altar stands at the far end.
lower_corridor: ^The corridor slopes deep underground. The air grows warmer.
right_alcove: ^A small alcove with an offering bowl.
south_east_junction: ^A junction. Passages lead east and south.
south_west_junction: ^A junction. Passages lead west and to a staircase.
carving_study: ^The carvings describe the pyramid's original purpose — a place of communication with ancestors.
chamber_one_back: ^A smaller chamber with a spirit vessel on a pedestal.
shaft_bottom: ^The bottom of the shaft opens into a large underground chamber.
central_altar: ^The altar has a slot for a sigil and a bowl for an offering.
north_chamber: ^A chamber to the north of the central hall.
north_chamber_search: ^Old ritual objects and dust. Nothing immediately useful.
south_chamber: ^A chamber to the south. Cooler than the rest.
deep_level: ^The deepest accessible level. Ancient and intact.
lower_side_room: ^A small room off the lower corridor.
east_wing: ^The east wing of the pyramid. Multiple rooms.
south_exterior_room: ^A room opening onto the south exterior.
west_wing: ^The west wing. Darker than the east.
upper_level_west: ^The upper west level. Light filters through cracks in the ceiling.
vessel_examination: ^The vessel is empty but beautiful. Ancient craftsmanship.
underground_chamber: ^A vast underground chamber. Columns and carvings everywhere.
underground_north: ^The northern part of the underground level.
altar_study: ^The altar is finely crafted. The slot and bowl are clearly designed for specific items.
offering_room: ^A room dedicated to ritual offerings. A carved bowl sits on a pedestal.
offering_room_study: ^The room depicts the proper ritual for the central altar.
south_connecting: ^A connecting passage to the south wing.
deep_west: ^The western deep level. A sigil chamber lies ahead.
deep_east: ^The eastern deep level. Very old structures here.
east_room_one: ^A storage room. Old crates and jars.
east_room_two: ^A smaller ritual room. An offering on a pedestal.
east_wing_end: ^The end of the east wing. A collapsed doorway.
south_exterior: ^The south exterior of the pyramid. Jungle presses close.
west_end_chamber: ^A large chamber at the end of the west wing. A spirit presence is felt.
upper_west_rooms: ^Several rooms on the upper west level.
upper_east_passage: ^A passage connecting the upper west to the upper east.
underground_central: ^The central structure underground. An old mechanism.
sigil_chamber: ^A chamber with a large sigil carved into the floor.
deep_east_explore: ^Very ancient structures. Pre-dating the rest of the pyramid.
deep_east_passage: ^A passage leading to an underground pool.
spirit_meeting: ^A spirit speaks, describing the ritual needed to open the inner sanctum.
spirit_guidance: ^The spirit confirms: sigil and offering, placed on the central altar.
upper_east_level: ^The upper east level. Views down into the central hall.
deepest_level: ^The absolute deepest point. Ancient beyond imagining.
sigil_study: ^The sigil represents the connection between living and dead.
underground_pool: ^A still underground pool. Reflections on the ceiling.
south_wing_end: ^The end of the south wing. A staircase leads up.
deepest_exploration: ^Primordial carvings. The earliest inhabitants left their mark here.
pool_reflection: ^The reflections show images of the past. Unsettling.
upper_south_level: ^The upper south level. Connects to the upper west.
upper_south_explore: ^A series of rooms with painted walls.
upper_south_paintings: ^Vibrant paintings depicting daily life in the ancient complex.
inner_sanctum: ^The innermost sanctum. The heart of the pyramid.
sanctum_exploration: ^Ancient artefacts and the preserved remains of the pyramid's last occupants.
pyramid_exit: ^A passage leads out of the pyramid into the jungle beyond.
obsidian_maze: ^A maze of obsidian walls. Every passage looks identical.
flooded_shaft: ^A shaft completely filled with water. No way through.
sunken_temple: ^A sunken temple beneath the pyramid complex.
hidden_barracks: ^Hidden barracks for the pyramid's ancient guards.
outer_temple_ring: ^A ring of standing stones surrounding the temple complex.
stone_ring_east: ^The eastern section of the stone ring. Carvings face sunrise.
stone_ring_north: ^The northern section. These stones are taller.
tall_stone_study: ^A faint inscription winds around the stone. Untranslatable but clearly significant.
stone_examination: ^The stone is warm to the touch despite the cool air.
stone_warmth: ^A faint vibration, almost inaudible. Ancient energy.
offering_terrace: ^A broad terrace outside the temple where offerings were made.
terrace_search: ^Stone vessels and offering fragments. Ancient remnants.
terrace_deep_search: ^Beneath some fragments you find a small carved token.
fire_pit_examination: ^A large circular fire pit. Cold ash, centuries old.
ash_search: ^Charred bone and old coins. Votive offerings burned long ago.
terrace_altar: ^A flat altar stone with a channel carved around its edge.
channel_examination: ^The channel would have carried blood during sacrifices. Ancient and solemn.
west_shrine: ^A small shrine set into the western hillside.
shrine_approach: ^A carved niche with a faded statue inside.
shrine_statue: ^A deity figure, worn almost smooth. Still powerful in presence.
shrine_offerings: ^Fresh offerings — flowers and food. Someone has been here recently.
shrine_panel: ^A carved stone panel depicting a mythological scene.
panel_study: ^The scene shows a figure descending into the earth and returning with fire.
eastern_annex: ^An annex on the eastern side of the temple.
annex_main_space: ^A large room, once used for preparation rituals.
annex_main_search: ^Ritual equipment — bowls, censers, and candle holders.
annex_inner: ^A smaller inner room. Private rituals happened here.
annex_inner_search: ^Personal effects of a priest or priestess. A carved bone stylus.
annex_niches: ^Storage niches carved into the walls. Most empty.
niche_search_result: ^You find some sealed ceramic vessels in the deepest niche.
ceramic_vessel: ^Dried herbs and resin inside. Incense material.
underground_stream: ^An underground stream running beneath the temple complex.
stream_source: ^The stream emerges from a crack in the rock. Cold and clear.
stream_crack: ^The crack goes deeper than you can see. A draught comes from within.
stream_outlet: ^The stream disappears under a low arch. The passage continues.
stream_arch: ^Too low to follow further without getting wet.
stream_bed_search: ^Smooth stones and sand. You find some coloured pebbles.
ritual_preparation_hall: ^A large hall used to prepare priests for ceremonies.
dressing_alcoves: ^Alcoves where ritual garments were stored and donned.
garment_remnants: ^Fragments of ancient cloth and some bronze pins.
bathing_pool: ^A shallow pool fed by the underground stream. Used for ritual purification.
pool_examination: ^The water is still fresh. The drain mechanism still functions.
preparation_inner: ^An inner sanctum for the head priest's preparation.
preparation_inner_search: ^A private altar and personal ritual items. A sealed clay tablet.
clay_tablet: ^The tablet is inscribed with what appear to be instructions for a ceremony.
north_passage_temple: ^A passage running along the northern side of the temple.
east_passage_temple: ^The eastern end of the north passage. It connects back to the main hall.
passage_doors_north: ^Three doors along the north passage. All slightly ajar.
north_room_a: ^A small room with a single window. Empty.
north_room_b: ^A room with collapsed shelving. Books rotted away.
north_room_b_search: ^A bronze clasp from a book cover, beautifully made.
north_room_c: ^A locked room. The lock is old but solid.
ceremonial_courtyard: ^An inner courtyard used for open-air ceremonies.
^Try the gate at the far end
courtyard_perimeter: ^Stone benches for spectators line the walls.
central_dais: ^A raised platform at the centre. The focal point of ceremonies.
dais_standing: ^From up here the whole courtyard is visible. The sight lines are deliberate.
dais_carvings: ^The platform edges are carved with repeated symbols.
courtyard_far_gate: ^A gate at the far end of the courtyard. Leads deeper into the complex.
treasury_temple: ^The temple treasury. Shelves stripped long ago.
treasury_search_temple: ^Empty shelves and dust. Whatever was here is long gone.
treasury_locked_box: ^A heavy box bolted to the floor. No keyhole visible.
box_examination: ^A pressure mechanism — it opens when weight is applied in a specific pattern.
treasury_hidden: ^You find a hidden compartment in the wall. Empty.
temple_exit: ^The exit from the temple complex. Daylight ahead.
priests_quarters: ^The priests' residential quarters beside the temple.
priests_main_room: ^A study and reception area. Books and administrative records.
priests_sleeping: ^Sleeping mats and personal effects. Simple and austere.
crypt_tombs: ^Stone tombs of high priests. Inscriptions name them all.
tomb_inscriptions: ^Names and titles going back centuries. The lineage of high priests.
sealed_crypt_chamber: ^A chamber sealed with wax and carved warnings.
crypt_warnings: ^The warnings describe the dangers of disturbing the dead. Standard priestly intimidation.
crypt_far_passage: ^A passage leading further under the temple.
deep_crypt_passage: ^The passage descends steeply. The air grows cold.
west_courtyard: ^A secondary courtyard on the western side.
west_courtyard_search: ^Old pots and garden tools. Once a working space.
west_courtyard_door: ^A door into the western section of the temple.
ablution_fountain: ^A fountain for ritual hand washing at the temple entrance.
fountain_mechanism: ^A clever gravity-fed system. Still functional after all these centuries.
fountain_use: ^Cold water flows over your hands. Ritualistic but refreshing.
sacred_grove: ^A grove of ancient trees considered sacred. Outside the main complex.
grove_walk: ^The trees are enormous, centuries old. The light through the leaves is extraordinary.
oldest_tree: ^A vast tree, clearly the oldest. Offerings hang from its branches.
tree_offerings: ^Cloth strips, carved figures, small mirrors. Diverse traditions.
grove_offerings: ^Small items left at tree bases — coins, flowers, food.
outer_gatehouse: ^The outer gatehouse of the temple complex.
guard_room_t: ^A room for the temple guards. Weapons racks, now empty.
guard_room_search_t: ^You find a bronze seal in a crack in the floor.
gate_mechanism_t: ^A counterweight system for operating the heavy gates.
pilgrim_way: ^The pilgrim approach road to the temple. Lined with stones.
pilgrim_stones: ^Marker stones showing distance and bearing. Ancient waymarkers.
pilgrim_rest: ^A covered rest area for pilgrims on the approach road.
pilgrim_rest_search: ^Benches and a cold hearth. Someone left a blanket.
water_channel: ^An ancient water channel bringing water to the temple.
channel_source: ^A spring emerging from the hillside. The source of the temple's water.
channel_examination_t: ^Precisely cut stone carrying water over a long distance. Impressive engineering.
votive_deposit_pit: ^A pit where votive offerings were deposited.
pit_edge_search: ^Near the surface you find some coins and a clay figurine.
rock_cut_tomb: ^A tomb cut directly into the rock face near the temple.
rock_tomb_interior: ^A small chamber with a stone sarcophagus. Sealed and undisturbed.
rock_tomb_inscription: ^The name and titles of a high priest from the early period of the temple.
sarcophagus_exam: ^The sarcophagus lid is carved with a portrait of the occupant.
lower_east_temple: ^The lower eastern section of the temple, partially underground.
lower_east_rooms: ^Several small rooms for storage and minor rituals.
lower_east_search: ^Ritual vessels and stone tools. Everyday temple equipment.
drainage_system: ^An elaborate drainage system running beneath the temple.
high_altar_chamber: ^The main high altar chamber of the temple.
high_altar_approach: ^The altar is massive and ancient. Stains from centuries of offerings.
outer_enclosure: ^The outer enclosure wall of the temple complex. A boundary between sacred and profane.
bridge_descent: ^A descent to the rope bridge exit. Your rope snags on a rock and tears.
rope_bridge_exit: ^A rope bridge across a ravine — the only way out.
^Use your rope to secure the crossing
